## Adding or changing a site? (delete if not)

Editing the registry lives in the importer, not the generated file —
`adler-core/data/sites.json` is regenerated by `scripts/import_sherlock.py`,
so durable changes go in its `OVERRIDES` / `KNOWN_BROKEN` maps. See
[CONTRIBUTING.md](../blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#adding-or-fixing-a-site).

- [ ] `url` uses `{username}` and prefers a **stable API/feed endpoint** over a
      bot-protected canonical page (e.g. an oEmbed/RSS/public-API URL — these
      don't get Cloudflare-walled).
- [ ] `known_present` is set to a **real existing account** so the doctor can
      verify the positive case.
- [ ] I ran `adler --doctor --only "<site>"` and it reports `[OK]`
      (or I've noted below why it can't from my network).
- [ ] If detection was non-obvious, I used `adler --doctor --fix --only "<site>"`
      to derive the signature.

`adler --doctor` output (paste the relevant lines):

```
```

> The `validate-sites` check will re-run the schema + loader invariants (hard)
> and a doctor pass on your changed sites (advisory — CI runs from a datacenter
> IP, so bot-protected sites may show Uncertain there even when correct).
